channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Amex Is Upgrading Resy to Win the Restaurant Reservation Wars Once and for All
item.description: American Express is folding Tock into Resy, creating a single, 25,000 venue-strong giant to drive consumer spending and credit card loyalty and leave ...

item.title: This Protein Could Help Keep Your Brain Young, Study Says
item.description: Scientists revealed how the protein can be used to rejuvenates neural stem cells.

item.title: The BAFTA Tourette racial slur controversy, explained, by a sociologist with Tourette’s who studies social stigma
item.description: Tourette syndrome remains a deeply misunderstood condition, especially when it comes to obscene language tics, called coprolalia.

item.title: The remote work fight isn’t over: Workers are willing to take a major pay cut, up to 25%, Harvard study shows
item.description: Harvard, Brown, and UCLA researchers found workers would be willing to take a massive pay cut to work from home.

item.title: World Economic Forum CEO steps down after appearing in the Epstein files, marking more turmoil for WEF
item.description: Yet another powerful person has stepped down after being named in the Epstein files. Børge Brende, president and CEO of the World Economic Forum (WEF)...

item.title: Sign Up Now: Meet Gary Vaynerchuk and Poppi’s Allison Ellsworth in This Exclusive Inc. Livestream Today at 2 p.m. ET
Inconvenient fact about the Epstein files: they’re missing Trump mentions that have appeared in the press
item.description: “Several individuals and news outlets have recently flagged files ... that they claim appear to be missing,” the Justice Department said in a post on ...

item.title: MasterClass Is Working With OpenAI to Build a Business School for the AI Era
item.description: The new program, which also involves the University of Chicago, is called MasterClass Executive. Mark Cuban is among the instructors.

item.title: Why Pennsylvania sits at the center of the big data-center power problem
item.description: Its old, slow and sits at the center of both the midAtlantic and Midwestern regional grids. What could go wrong?

item.title: This charming pixel art game solves one of AI coding’s most annoying UX problems
item.description: Everyone who has tried to code with Anthropic’s Claude Code AI agents runs into the same usability problem: If you run two or three concurrent artific...

item.title: The great (small business) wealth transfer: McKinsey sees $5 trillion of baby boomer companies coming up for sale over the next decade
item.description: Move over, Great Wealth Transfer. The McKinsey Institute for Economic Mobility sees the next decade as make or break for the Great Ownership Transfer.

item.title: Nvidia’s quarterly results exceed projections as concerns mount over AI economy
item.description: Artificial intelligence chipmaker Nvidia on Wednesday announced another quarter of astounding quarterly growth as investors try to decipher whether te...

item.title: Punch the monkey and his stuffed orangutan toy are taking over Etsy, Amazon, and Ikea with merch
item.description: There are few things that unite the world like animal videos. There are also few things that are so readily commoditized. Both have occurred in the ca...

item.title: OpenAI wants to get the government hooked on ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI has emerged as one of the government’s leading providers of artificial intelligence . According to the company, 37 federal agencies now have ac...

item.title: How Much Is Squatty Potty Worth? Founder’s Disturbing Arrest Shines Spotlight on ‘Shark Tank’ Product
item.description: Robert Edwards pleaded not guilty to federal charges of receipt of child sexual abuse material.
